subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Identify chemical changes in daily life
Write and balance simple chemical equations
Understand and identify basic types of chemical reactions
Observe the effects of oxidation in everyday life (corrosion and rancidity)
Recognize the characteristics of chemical reactions like gas evolution or color change

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Chemical Reactions
topics_covered: Observations that determine a chemical reaction
student_activities: List five chemical changes seen at home
Observe and record changes during the Magnesium ribbon experiment
assessment: Classifying a list of changes as Physical or Chemical

lesson_plan.title: Chemical Equations
topics_covered: Representing reactions using symbols and formulae
teaching_strategy: Relating symbols to elements from the Periodic Table
student_activities: Convert word equations into symbol equations
Practice writing formulae for common compounds

lesson_plan.title: Balancing Chemical Equations
topics_covered: Hit and trial method for balancing
Making equations more informative (states of matter)
student_activities: Identify the physical states (s, l, g, aq) in given reactions

lesson_plan.title: Combination and Decomposition Reactions
topics_covered: Definition and examples of Combination reactions
teaching_strategy: Visual aids/Videos of decomposition of Lead Nitrate
student_activities: Draw a diagram for the electrolysis of water
assessment: Exit slip: Name one decomposition reaction used in daily life

lesson_plan.title: Displacement and Double Displacement
topics_covered: Displacement reactions (Iron nail in Copper Sulphate)
teaching_strategy: Live experiment/Demonstration of color change in solution
student_activities: Observe and note the change in color of Iron nails
Predict if a reaction will occur based on a simple reactivity chart

lesson_plan.title: Oxidation, Reduction, and Corrosion
topics_covered: Definition of Oxidation and Reduction (Oxygen gain/loss)
teaching_strategy: Samples of rusted iron and spoiled food packets (chips)
Discussion on how to prevent food spoilage
student_activities: Identify which substance is oxidized in a simple equation
assessment: Poster/Slogan on 'Preventing Food Waste and Rancidity'

teaching_aids: Science Lab Kit (Magnesium ribbon, Copper Sulphate, Iron nails)
Samples of rusted objects and nitrogen-flushed food packets
teacher_tip: Focus heavily on the visual changes (color change, gas bubbles) to help students 'see' the chemistry before they try to 'write' the chemistry. Keep balancing practice limited to small coefficients initially to build confidence.
